mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, triggering immediate negative short-term equity repricing.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate compression that boosts equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The real rate differential shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a cumulative ICIR below 0.5 across all market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust economic expansion that immediately boosts market sentiment and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: GDP releases above the 3.0% threshold fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns, with a win rate below 40% across 50+ data releases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising unemployment during a recessionary phase confirms deepening economic contraction, forcing negative equity repricing over the next month.
failure_condition: The negative divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 55% of recessionary observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.
